channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: StudentAid.gov website went down, following mass layoffs at Department of Education
item.description: An hourslong outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Forget Apple Intelligence, for the enterprise there’s webAI
item.description: Shrouded behind the mists of Apple Intelligence, one of the more thought-provoking implementations of artificial intelligence for real-world business use doesn’t come from Apple; it comes from webAI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ducks were once a conservation bright spot. Now they're declining in the US, new report shows
item.description: A new report says at least 112 North American bird species have lost more than half their populations in the past 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dangerous Chrome extensions mimic password managers
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy explains how to protect yourself from malicious Chrome extensions that can steal sensitive information like password managers, crypto wallets and banking apps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Administration Cuts Hinder Implementation of SHARE IT Act Software Mandates
item.description: The Trump administration's cuts to the federal corps of civic technology experts could hinder implementation of the SHARE IT Act software mandates, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal Trade Commission Seeks Delay in Amazon Prime Subscription Case
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission has asked a federal judge to delay a trial in a case accusing Amazon of using deceptive practices in its Prime subscription program due to staffing and budgetary challenges at the government agency.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie finds a middle ground on tech policy during Trump’s second term
item.description: In our current political and media environment the loudest voices are the ones that are farthest from the center. Tech hasn’t been spared, with some Silicon Valley leaders drifting right—not only out of ideology, but also pragmatism.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: How the Pittsburgh Steelers leverage the Fortinet Security Fabric for industry-leading defense
item.description: The Pittsburgh Steelers are one of the most recognizable American football teams, with tens of millions of fans tuning into games and a busy stadium that can hold more than 68,000 fans.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk’s White House role weighs on business empire
item.description: Elon Musk’s role in the Trump administration is complicating his business ventures, as the tech billionaire pushes forward with efforts to slash the federal government and budget in the face of mounting criticism.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence
item.description: The U.S. Copyright Office completed the second of two studies of AI and copyrights as it dealt with whether you can copyright outputs created using AI. The post The Copyright Office takes on the sticky issue of artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hey Apple! When you’re in a hole, stop digging
item.description: Soon after the non-appearance of the most important Apple Intelligence feature, Apple now finds itself in danger of digging an even deeper hole by promising iOS 19 will be the most significant upgrade in years. The problem: it again runs the risk of promising too much and not delivering enough.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 of the best LLM integration tools for R
item.description: When we first looked at this space in late 2023, many generative AI R packages focused largely on OpenAI and ChatGPT-like functionality or coding add-ins. Today, the landscape includes packages that natively support more LLM suppliers—including models run locally on your own computer.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding
item.description: "The Trump administration's efforts to reduce the size of the federal government have resulted in decreased funding for biomedical research," said Sandra Babosu The post Trade groups urge the administration to maintain basic research funding first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Wyoming billionaire 1 of 4 in race to buy TikTok
item.description: Wyoming entrepreneur Reid Raisner told "NewsNation Live" on Monday that the fate of TikTok is a matter of U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Is that iPhone app spying? Apple’s App Privacy Report reveals all
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson urges you to use Apple’s App Privacy Report to see what your apps are really up to.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia GTC 2025: What to expect from the AI leader
item.description: No company has both driven and benefited from AI advancements more than Nvidia. Last year, Nvidia’s GTC 2024 grabbed headlines with its introduction of the Blackwell architecture and the DGX systems powered by it. With GTC 2025 right around the corner (it runs March 17- 21 in San Jose, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vibes won’t make your software successful
item.description: I regret to inform you that vibes don’t pay the bills. Specifically, open source vibes. For as long as I’ve followed open source, there has been a tendency for the industry to focus on normative values of open source, i.e.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 10 things developers love about JavaScript – and 10 things they don’t
item.description: It was 30 years ago today, Sgt. JavaScript taught the web to play. Well, not exactly but close enough. The programming language began in 1995 as a project in the depths of the browser maker Netscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Helping DOGE cut waste
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sovereign European Cloud API claims to offer interoperability without lock-in
item.description: Europe’s efforts to free itself from the domination of US cloud platforms have reached an important first milestone with the announcement of the Sovereign European Cloud API (SECA).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Stop carrying your physical driver's license and add it to your Apple Wallet
item.description: A digital ID may be even more secure than a physical ID. Storing your driver's license in your Apple Wallet is an ideal solution for commuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signs executive order establishing bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order Thursday establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency's journey towards possible mainstream acceptance. Under Trump's new order, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: "We have a lot of interest in TikTok. China is going to play a role, so hopefully China will approve of the deal," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4
